data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Supports Hungarian Prime Minister Orban's Re-election
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban's bid for re-election. According to Jin10, Trump praised Orban's leadership and policies, highlighting the strong bilateral relations between the United States and Hungary. Trump emphasized the importance of maintaining stability and continuity in Hungary's political landscape, suggesting that Orban's continued leadership would benefit both nations. The endorsement comes as Hungary prepares for its upcoming elections, with Orban seeking another term in office. Trump's backing is seen as a significant gesture, reflecting the close ties between the two leaders and their shared political views.

data.title: Airgas to Reduce Helium Shipments After Qatar Production Halt
data.description: Airgas, a leading U.S. distributor of packaged gases, announced it will reduce helium shipments. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows Qatar's suspension of operations at a significant liquefied natural gas facility due to an Iranian strike. The halt in production is expected to impact the global supply of helium, a critical component in various industries, including healthcare and technology. The situation underscores the vulnerability of supply chains to geopolitical tensions, particularly in regions with concentrated production facilities. Airgas is assessing the situation and exploring alternative sources to mitigate the impact on its customers.

data.title: Climate Scientist Resigns from NASA Amid Administrative Changes
data.description: Prominent climate scientist Kate Marvel has resigned from NASA, citing significant changes under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration as a key factor in her decision. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Marvel's concerns about the direction of climate science and policy during this period. Her departure underscores the challenges faced by scientists in navigating political shifts that impact research and environmental strategies. Marvel's resignation reflects broader concerns within the scientific community regarding the administration's approach to climate issues.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Short-Term Bounce Amid Conflicting Geopolitical Signals
data.description: Bitcoin and other risk assets have shown a short-term bounce following reports from Israeli media suggesting that Washington is advocating for a one-month pause in fighting. According to NS3.AI, these developments have led to mixed reactions in the financial markets, with stocks rising and oil prices falling. However, Iran has previously dismissed negotiation reports as false, which adds uncertainty to the situation. The response from Iran is seen as a crucial factor influencing near-term market sentiment.

data.title: Norway's Equinor CEO: New York Empire Wind Project to Begin Power Generation by Year-End
data.description: Norway's Equinor CEO has announced that the New York Empire Wind project is expected to achieve its first power generation by the end of the year. According to Jin10, the project is anticipated to be completed next year. This development marks a significant milestone in the expansion of renewable energy infrastructure in the United States, contributing to the country's clean energy goals.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Barr Suggests Interest Rates May Remain Unchanged Due to Inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Barr has indicated that interest rates might need to remain steady for some time due to inflation exceeding target levels. According to ChainCatcher, this statement highlights the ongoing challenges faced by the Federal Reserve in managing economic stability amid persistent inflationary pressures.

data.title: BYD's 'God's Eye' Feature Faces User Complaints Over Performance Issues
data.description: BYD's 'God's Eye' feature, designed to enhance driving experience, is reportedly underperforming according to user feedback. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that users have raised concerns about steering inaccuracies, navigational screen malfunctions, and delays. These issues have led to dissatisfaction among customers who expected a seamless integration of the feature into their driving routines. The 'God's Eye' feature, which was marketed as a cutting-edge technology, is now facing scrutiny as BYD works to address these performance challenges.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. 'Golden Dome' Project Software Development Underway by Anduril, Palantir, and Lockheed Martin
data.description: Anduril Industries, Palantir Technologies, and Lockheed Martin are actively developing software for the United States' 'Golden Dome' project, according to Jin10. This initiative aims to enhance national security through advanced technological solutions. The collaboration between these companies highlights the growing importance of integrating cutting-edge software in defense systems. The 'Golden Dome' project is expected to leverage artificial intelligence and data analytics to improve threat detection and response capabilities. This development underscores the strategic focus on utilizing AI-driven technologies to bolster defense infrastructure.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsbee: Uncertainty Over Rate Cuts Depends on War Duration
data.description: Federal Reserve official Austan Goolsbee has expressed uncertainty regarding potential interest rate cuts, stating that the decision hinges on the duration of ongoing conflicts. According to Jin10, Goolsbee highlighted the unpredictable nature of geopolitical tensions and their impact on economic policy. He emphasized that the Federal Reserve is closely monitoring the situation to determine the appropriate course of action. The ongoing war has introduced significant variables into economic forecasting, making it challenging to predict future monetary policy adjustments. Goolsbee's comments reflect the broader concerns within the Federal Reserve about the implications of prolonged geopolitical instability on the U.S. economy. The central bank remains vigilant in assessing the evolving situation and its potential effects on financial markets and economic growth.

data.title: Fed Governor Barr: Evidence of Inflation Decline Needed Before Rate Cuts
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr stated that evidence of a decline in inflation is necessary before considering interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, Barr emphasized the importance of monitoring inflation trends closely to ensure that any policy adjustments align with the central bank's objectives. The Federal Reserve continues to focus on achieving its dual mandate of stable prices and maximum employment, and any decision to alter interest rates will be contingent upon clear signs of inflationary pressures easing. Barr's comments highlight the cautious approach the Fed is taking in response to current economic conditions.

data.title: OpenAI CFO: Preparing for IPO with Current Funding Round
data.description: OpenAI's Chief Financial Officer, Brett Freiler, emphasized the company's focus on preparing for a potential initial public offering (IPO). According to Jin10, Freiler stated that the current funding round aims to mitigate associated risks and ensure the company is ready for the public market. This strategic move highlights OpenAI's commitment to strengthening its financial position and operational readiness as it considers future growth opportunities.

data.title: Dollar Index Steady Amid Middle East Tensions and Oil Price Concerns
data.description:
The financial markets are closely monitoring developments in the Middle East and fluctuations in oil prices, with the dollar index remaining stable near the 99.2 level by the end of trading in New York. According to RTHK, the euro showed little change against the dollar, recently quoted at $1.1608. The British pound fell by 0.1% against the dollar, trading at $1.3412. Meanwhile, the dollar rose approximately 0.2% against the Japanese yen, reaching 158.7 yen.

U.S. Treasury yields increased, with weak demand observed in the auction of two-year Treasury notes. The yield on the two-year Treasury note peaked at 3.966%, rising over 13 basis points before easing to around 3.89%. The yield on the ten-year Treasury note reached a high of 4.427%, climbing more than 9 basis points, and later settled near 4.36%. Editor: Zhang Shimin

data.title: Forgent Plans to Sell 30 Million Shares Following Recent IPO
data.description: Forgent and certain shareholders have announced plans to sell a total of 30 million shares, coming shortly after the company's initial public offering. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the move as a significant development for the electrical equipment firm. The decision to offer additional shares follows less than two months after Forgent's IPO, indicating a strategic approach to capitalizing on market conditions. This sale could impact the company's stock performance and investor sentiment, as stakeholders assess the implications of increased share availability.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Pressure Amid Stagflation Concerns Following U.S. PMI Data
data.description: Renewed fears of stagflation are impacting Bitcoin, according to NS3.AI, following the release of flash U.S. March PMI data. The data revealed manufacturing at 52.4 and services at 51.1, suggesting a mixed economic outlook. This combination may reduce the likelihood of a Federal Reserve rate cut, thereby exerting pressure on risk assets such as Bitcoin.

data.title: Japanese Rural Banks Face Challenges Amid Population Decline
data.description: Japanese banks in rural areas are facing significant difficulties as the population continues to shrink. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the severe situation at a small credit union located at the northernmost tip of Japan. The demographic decline is impacting the financial stability of these institutions, leading to concerns about their future viability. As the population ages and decreases, banks are experiencing reduced demand for services, which is exacerbating their financial challenges. The situation underscores the broader economic issues facing rural Japan, where depopulation is affecting various sectors. Efforts to address these challenges are ongoing, but the outlook remains uncertain for many of these banks.

data.title: CME Data: Low Probability of Fed Rate Hike in April
data.description: According to Jin10, CME's "FedWatch" tool indicates a 9.3% probability of a 25 basis point rate hike by the Federal Reserve in April, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate stands at 90.7%. Looking ahead to June, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate hike is 17.1%, with an 80.2% chance of rates remaining unchanged.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Futures Rise with Nasdaq Futures Up Nearly 1%
data.description: U.S. stock futures showed an upward trend at the start of trading, with Nasdaq futures increasing by nearly 1%. According to Jin10, this movement reflects a positive sentiment in the market as investors anticipate upcoming economic data and corporate earnings reports. The rise in futures suggests optimism among traders, potentially driven by expectations of favorable outcomes in the financial markets. Market participants are closely monitoring these developments to gauge the direction of the U.S. economy and its impact on investment strategies.

data.title: 21Shares: Actively managed crypto ETPs will be the next stage of investment; global actively managed ETFs reach nearly $1.8 trillion.
data.description: Duncan Moir, President of 21Shares, stated that as the crypto market matures from simple price-tracking funds, actively managed exchange-traded products will become the next stage of crypto investment. Data compiled by Morningstar and Goldman Sachs Asset Management shows that global actively managed ETF assets will approach $1.8 trillion by the end of 2025. Moir pointed out that crypto, as an emerging and growing asset class, is particularly well-suited for active management; 21Shares combines bottom-up research and quantification of single assets with independent top-down strategies for risk and allocation management, and has expanded its portfolio management and trading teams. Moir added that the integration following FalconX's acquisition of 21Shares in October is expected to accelerate product development, especially in more complex products. Moir noted that demand for crypto ETPs and ETFs varies across regions; in Europe, where the investor base is more mature, institutions already holding Bitcoin and Ethereum are seeking to further increase their crypto allocations. Against this backdrop, 21Shares recently launched an exchange-traded product in Europe linked to Strategy Preferred Stock (STRC), offering exposure to high-yield instruments related to the company's Bitcoin capital strategy, and reported strong early demand in several regions. The report notes that as the crypto ETP and ETF markets develop, issuers are launching more complex structures, with staking becoming one of the growth directions; Grayscale introduced staking into its ETP in October, and BlackRock launched a Nasdaq-listed Ethereum product with a staking mechanism in March, recording $15.5 million in trading volume on its first day. Duncan Moir stated that 21Shares evaluates new products based on internal research, customer needs, and market trends, citing its Bitcoin and Gold ETPs as examples, noting that these products have been running for four years and recently cross-listed in London. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: UK Defense Ministry: Sovereign Base Areas Never Belonged to Cyprus Republic
data.description: The United Kingdom's Ministry of Defence has stated that the Sovereign Base Areas have never been part of the Republic of Cyprus. According to Jin10, the UK has no plans to alter this status. The Sovereign Base Areas, which are British Overseas Territories, have been a point of contention in the region. The UK maintains these areas for strategic military purposes, and their status has been a longstanding issue in UK-Cyprus relations. The statement reaffirms the UK's position on the matter, indicating no forthcoming changes to the current arrangement.

data.title: 21Shares President Predicts Growth in Actively Managed Crypto ETPs
data.description: Actively managed crypto exchange-traded products (ETPs) are poised to become a significant trend in the crypto investment landscape, according to 21Shares President Duncan Moir. According to NS3.AI, Moir highlighted that as global active ETF assets are projected to reach $1.8 trillion by the end of 2025, the crypto sector is particularly well-suited for active management. He noted that issuers are increasingly developing more complex products, including those with staking structures, to cater to evolving investor needs.
